title: delphinium occidentale var. cucullatum
intro: Delphinium occidentale var. cucullatum, also known as the Pacific larkspur, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and British Columbia, characterized by its tall spikes of blue or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Delphinium occidentale var. cucullatum include reducing inflammation, relieving muscle spasms, and acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, but caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential for side effects.
